feats.description: As your body continues to rot and putrefy, your senses deaden and your undead powers grow.

feats.name: Numb to Death
feats.description: Your past has left you numb to death's call.
feats.prerequisites: Diehard; you have died at least once

feats.name: Numbing Spice Exhalation
feats.description: You can mix alchemically preserved peppers into an alchemical food consumable to spice it up with alchemical fire.

feats.name: Oath of The Avenger
feats.description: You’ve sworn an oath to punish wicked acts you witness.

feats.name: Oath of The Defender
feats.description: During your daily preparations, you can swear an oath to defend against a certain kind of enemy during your deeds that day.

feats.name: Oath of The Slayer
feats.description: During your daily preparations, you can swear an oath to defeat, topple, or destroy a certain kind of enemy during your deeds that day.

feats.name: Oatia Skysage Dedication
feats.description: Your study of the stars unlocks supernatural insights and magic.

feats.name: Obscured Emergence
feats.description: You are, by nature, a thing of gloomy woods and barren wilds, and when you choose show yourself, a bit of this gloom clings to you, obscuring your form with strange distortions or mist.

feats.name: Obscured Terrain
feats.description: When you cast obscuring mist, you can modify the spell to blanket the area, making it difficult to see the ground.
feats.prerequisites: Spell Trickster Dedication; ability to cast obscuring mist

feats.name: Observational Analysis
feats.description: You are able to rapidly discern relevant details about your opponents in the heat of combat.

feats.name: Occult Breadth
feats.description: Your repertoire expands, and you can cast more occult spells each day.

feats.name: Occult Dragonblood
feats.description: Your blood contains a tiny fragment of unusual or inexplicable power from a mysterious dragon, such as a conspirator dragon or omen dragon.

feats.name: Occult Evolution
feats.description: You draw power from the obscure secrets of the universe.

feats.name: Occult Resistance
feats.description: Your study of occult practices has shown you how to best defend against them.

feats.name: Ocean Wariness
feats.description: Stories about those who have mistreated your people in the past (particularly stories about alghollthu mind control) left you untrusting of those outside your community.

feats.name: Ocean's Balm
feats.description: A blessing of the living sea salves wounds and douses flames.

feats.name: Ocean's Bite
feats.description: Your jaws open wider than a humanoid mouth should, showing off multiple rows of serrated shark teeth or a viperfish-like nest of needles.
feats.prerequisites: Abyssal Merfolk heritage or Carcharodon Merfolk heritage

feats.name: Oddity Identification
feats.description: Trained in Occultism
feats.campaign: Recognize magic involving minds, fortune, or secrets

feats.name: Ode to Ouroboros
feats.description: You learn the ode to ouroboros composition spell (page 371), which enables you to temporarily spare your allies from death.

feats.name: Of Lions and Wyrms
feats.description: You starred in a war drama depicting First Emperor Taldaris’s battles against the Grogrisant, a massive six- eyed lion, and Verksaris the Kingeater, a mighty dragon.

feats.name: Offensive Analysis
feats.description: You can enhance your senses and mental state to consider every aspect of an attack.

feats.name: Offensive Odor
feats.description: You've mastered your defensive pheromones and have nearly complete control over them.

feats.name: Offensive Subroutine
feats.description: Nanites augment your attacks.

feats.name: Officer's Education
feats.description: You know that a broad knowledge base is critical for a competent commander.

feats.name: Officer's Expertise
feats.description: Your proficiency in commander class DC increases to expert, and you become an expert in Warfare Lore.

feats.name: Officer's Mastery
feats.description: Your proficiency in commander class DC increases to master, and you become a master in Warfare Lore.

feats.name: Officer's Medical Training
feats.description: You received formal training in battlefield triage and wound treatment.

feats.name: Officer's Protection
feats.description: When you use Commitment to Protection, you can target all allies within 30 feet.

feats.name: Oil Fire
feats.description: Your armor includes flame-resistant gauntlets with oil-filled finger joints.

feats.name: Old Soul
feats.description: Your immortal lineage has granted you a lifespan much longer than most mortals, and you've learned a lot along the way.

feats.name: Olethros's Decree
feats.description: You have a connection to olethroses, the enigmatic psychopomps who successfully swayed the yamaraj to permit the creation of the first duskwalkers.

feats.name: Omnikinesis
feats.description: You can realign your kinetic gate with a thought.

feats.name: On Borrowed Time
feats.description: You accelerate a foe's personal timestream, which causes them to experience time at a faster rate, putting a terrible strain on their mind and body.

feats.name: One For All
feats.description: With precisely the right words of encouragement, you bolster an ally's efforts.

feats.name: One More Activation
feats.description: You've forged a deeper bond to your invested items, allowing you to activate them more than usual.

feats.name: One with Earth
feats.description: You've learned to harness your connection to the earth to travel through it.

feats.name: One with the Land
feats.description: Your connection to your bound terrain strengthens, making you unhindered by local weather.

feats.name: One with the Spirits
feats.description: The spirits you’ve invoked throughout your life have left behind echoes and spiritual fragments, which you can manifest across your own flesh, temporarily transforming you into something more akin to spirit than mortal.

feats.name: One with the Wild
feats.description: You naturally blend in with the wilderness, becoming camouflaged.
feats.source: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 70

feats.name: One-inch Punch
feats.description: You put all your force into a single mighty, carefully controlled blow.
notes: Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers pg. 217, Hellbreakers Players Guide pg. 9
You aren’t above using underhanded tactics to gain an advantage, easily routing your opponents when they attack.

feats.name: One-millimeter Punch
feats.description: Your punches have incredible force and control.

feats.name: One-on-One
feats.description: Your true skill can be seen in a one-on-one fight where you have room to dance around your opponent.
feats.prerequisites: Aldori Duelist Dedication; Trained in Deception

feats.name: One-Toed Hop
feats.description: Assuming a peculiar stance, you make a short hop on each toe.

feats.name: Oneiric Influence
feats.description: While Infiltrating a Dream, you can also implant a suggestion in the target's mind.

feats.name: Ongoing Investigation
feats.description: You're always investigating what's around you, even as you perform other activities.

feats.name: Ongoing Selfishness
feats.description: Your powerful personality and incredible ego demand that you protect yourself above all else.

feats.name: Ongoing Strategy
feats.description: You're constantly studying small aspects of everyone's movements, even if you don't have a stratagem in place ahead of time.

feats.name: Oni Form
feats.description: Your horns flash briefly as you grow in size and ferocity.

feats.name: Oni Rampage
feats.description: Nothing invigorates you more than your own freshly spilled blood.

feats.name: Oni Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: Oni prefer large, cruel weapons for smashing their foes to pieces, and so do you.

feats.name: Oni's Mask
feats.description: You aren’t as malleable as your oni ancestors but still can disguise yourself.

feats.name: Only My Doom May Claim Me
feats.description: The power of your legend, fueled by your legendary deeds, resists any weapon that is not tied to your curse.

feats.name: Only the Worthy
feats.description: You can designate your ikons as movable by only your own hand, leaving them fixed in place as surely as if they were lodged in stone.

feats.name: Ooze Empathy
feats.description: You can connect with oozes (and other amorphous aberrations, at the GM's discretion) on a rudimentary level.
feats.prerequisites: Oozemorph Dedication; at least two other class feats from the oozemorph archetype

feats.name: Oozemorph Dedication
feats.description: You are plagued by sporadic anatomical rearrangements, which grant you insights into other creatures with unusual anatomies.

feats.name: Open Mind
feats.description: You have inherent psychic abilities that allow you to tap into the power of your mind.

feats.name: Open the Blazing Eye
feats.description: Thanks to your diligence in the study of divine and occult mysteries, you can focus to temporarily attain a perception beyond the ordinary.

feats.name: Opening Stance
feats.description: At the first sign of trouble, often before you consciously realize the danger, you drop into a stance with a mere thought.

feats.name: Opening Stance (Fighter)
feats.description: At the first sign of trouble, often before you consciously realize the danger, you drop into a stance with a mere thought.

feats.name: Operatic Adventurer
feats.description: Your training has focused you for the opera, and it certainly shows.

feats.name: Opportune Backstab
feats.description: When your enemy is hit by your ally, you capitalize upon the distraction.

feats.name: Opportune Opening
feats.description: Your combat training taught you to capitalize on any moment of weakness from your foe.

feats.name: Opportune Throw
feats.description: Make a ranged attack with a Juggled item against a vulnerable creature.
feats.source: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 74
feats.source_book: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

feats.name: Oracle Dedication
feats.description: Choose a mystery.

feats.name: Oracular Providence
feats.description: Your mystery grants you access to deep reserves of truly miraculous divine power.

feats.name: Oracular Warning
feats.description: You have a premonition about impending danger that you use to warn your allies.

feats.name: Orator's Fillibuster
feats.description: When you seek to convince others of the worth of your cause, you are capable of unleashing such an entrancing torrent of rhetoric and metaphor that it is all but impossible to look away from you.

feats.name: Orc Ferocity
feats.description: Fierceness in battle runs through your blood, and you refuse to fall from your injuries no matter how terrible they may be.

feats.name: Orc Lore
feats.description: The hold elders taught you your people’s histories, told tales of great athletic feats, and shared with you the hardships your ancestors endured so that you can pass this wisdom down to future generations.

feats.name: Orc Sight
feats.description: Your orc blood grants you the keen vision of your forebears.

feats.name: Orc Superstition
feats.description: You defend yourself against magic by relying on techniques derived from orc cultural superstitions.

feats.name: Orc Warmask
feats.description: You paint your face to create a warmask.

feats.name: Orc Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: In combat, you favor the brutal weapons that are traditional for your orc ancestors.

feats.name: Orchard's Endurance
feats.description: Patches of bark appear on the skin of you and your nearby allies.

feats.name: Order Cross-Training
feats.description: You gain the lesser order benefit of a Hellknight order other than the one to which you belong.

feats.name: Order Explorer
feats.description: You have learned the secrets of another druidic order, passing whatever rites of initiation that order requires and gaining access to its secrets.

feats.name: Order Magic
feats.description: You have delved deeper into the teaching of a new order, gaining access to a coveted order spell.

feats.name: Order Spell
feats.description: You gain the initial order spell from your order.

feats.name: Order Training
feats.description: You gain the lesser order benefit for the Hellknight order to which you belong.

feats.name: Order Training (Uncommon)
feats.description: You gain the lesser order benefit for the Hellknight order to which you belong.

feats.name: Ore Fists
feats.description: When you enter the Stonestrike Stance, your fists appear to be covered with veins of unrefined ore.
feats.source: Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown pg. 82

feats.name: Orichalcum Bond
feats.description: The ancient Thassilonians associated sins with different skymetals, and you’ve adapted that theory to empower your bonded weapon beyond its normal limits.

feats.name: Ornate Tattoo
feats.description: You expand your tattoos to encompass greater magic.

feats.name: Ostentatious Arrival
feats.description: If the next action you take is to Manifest your Eidolon as a three-action activity, or to Cast a three-action summoning Spell, the creature appears in an explosion.

feats.name: Ostentatious Reload
feats.description: Every good Firebrand knows that style can be as important as substance—so long as you don't screw up the delivery.

feats.name: Ostilli Host Dedication
feats.description: You've bonded with an attached symbiote known as an ostilli.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Arcana or Trained in Nature
notes: Your senses speed up to the point that time seems to stand still, allowing you to catch details and incoming attacks.

feats.name: Otherworldly Acumen
feats.description: The arcane magic you possess grows in power and complexity.
feats.prerequisites: at least one innate spell gained from an elf ancestry feat

feats.name: Otherworldly Magic
feats.description: Your elven magic manifests as a simple arcane spell, even if you aren't formally trained in magic.

feats.name: Ouroboric Pact
feats.description: You’ve studied pacts at Cobyslarni, or learned under someone who has, and understand them to not only create a bargain between individuals, but a separate entity altogether: the pact itself.

feats.name: Out of Hand
feats.description: Even when your limbs are severed, they remain a part of you.

feats.name: Overcrowd
feats.description: As long as you are Small, you can end your movement in the same square as a Small ally.

feats.name: Overdrive Ally
feats.description: You quickly fling some of your powered-up mechanisms to an ally, sharing your benefits with them briefly.

feats.name: Overextending Feint
feats.description: You goad a foe into overextending.

feats.name: Overlooked Mastermind
feats.description: Many consider half-orcs little more than dumb brutes.

feats.name: Overpowering Charge
feats.description: You trample foes as you charge past.
feats.source: Player Core pg. 148, Player Core 2 pg. 82

feats.name: Oversized Throw
feats.description: With a great heave, you seize a piece of your surroundings, such as a boulder, log, table, wagon, or chunk of earth, and hurl it at your foes.

feats.name: Overwatch Dedication
feats.description: You have an innate knack for tactical observation, and you've innovated to apply that knack in a variety of ways that allow you to assist your allies.

feats.name: Overwhelming Blow
feats.description: You throw your weight into a powerful attack that leaves you vulnerable.

feats.name: Overwhelming Breath
feats.description: You control your breathing and align it with your qi, exhaling with controlled force in order to overcome your opponent's resistance.

feats.name: Overwhelming Energy
feats.description: With a complex gesture, you call upon the primal power of your spell to overcome enemies’ resistances.
feats.source: Player Core pg. 133, Player Core 2 pg. 155

feats.name: Overwhelming Spellstrike
feats.description: You pierce through your foe's resistance.

feats.name: Pack Attack
feats.description: You have mastered techniques for hunting alongside your pack.

feats.name: Pack Hunter
feats.description: You were taught how to hunt as part of a pack.

feats.name: Pack Movement
feats.description: You and your companion move to take down a common enemy.

feats.name: Pack Performance
feats.description: When you and animals sing together, your words ring even brighter and bolder, cutting through disruptions.

feats.name: Pack Rat
feats.description: You've learned how to cram vast quantities into small spaces.

feats.name: Pack Stalker
feats.description: Ambushes are an honored kholo tradition.

feats.name: Pack Tactics
feats.description: You have mastered how to hunt with your pack.

feats.name: Pack Takedown
feats.description: The pack is strongest when it acts as one, and like a group of hungry wolves, you work together to bring down a single target.

feats.name: Packed with Flavor
feats.description: Edible morsels you create are particularly potent and bursting with flavor.
feats.prerequisites: Wandering Chef Dedication; Expert in Crafting or Cooking Lore

feats.name: Pact of Draconic Fury
feats.description: You've sworn a pact with a dragon, giving you the ability to tap into the dragon's immeasurable power in exchange for adding to their hoard.

feats.name: Pact of Eldritch Eyes
feats.description: You've sworn a pact with an entity beyond mortal comprehension, granting you distant visions.

feats.name: Pact of Fey Glamour
feats.description: You've sworn a pact with fey powers, granting you the otherworldly appearance of your dreams in exchange for oaths of hospitality.

feats.name: Pact of Huldra's Renewal
feats.description: You’ve sworn a pact with Zemnaïdé, a huldra professor at Cobyslarni, to help renew his former homeland of the Sarkoris Scar.

feats.name: Pact of Infernal Prowess
feats.description: You've sworn a pact with devils, granting you success in life in exchange for your soul in the afterlife.

feats.name: Pact of the Crossroads
feats.description: You’ve sworn a pact with Ng the Hooded, the Eldest known as the Lord of the Crossroads, though your only sign of Ng’s acknowledgment is the benefits you receive.

feats.name: Pact of the Death Hunter
feats.description: You’ve sworn a pact with a psychopomp bent on the destruction of all undeath.

feats.name: Pact of the Fey Paths
feats.description: You’ve sworn a pact with a powerful fey creature that longs to see the wonders of the Universe.

feats.name: Pact of the Final Breath
feats.description: You've sworn a pact with a powerful psychopomp—typically an olethros advocate or yamaraj judge—granting you a reprieve before your soul will be collected in exchange for respect for the dead and assistance against the undead.

feats.name: Pact of the Living Pact
feats.description: You’ve sworn a pact with a pact that has somehow survived long after the death of the original pactbinder.

feats.name: Pact of the Nightblossom
feats.description: You’ve sworn a pact with Jenway Nightblossom of the Synod of Truth in Dreams, a talented oneiromancer and pactbinder.

feats.name: Pact of the Rune Dragon
feats.description: While the students of Cobyslarni are taught to make pacts with many kinds of dragons, a pact with a rune dragon is somewhat more frequent due to their interest in the accomplishments of various academies.

feats.name: Pactbinder Dedication
feats.description: The first step toward forming a successful pact is an understanding of the magic behind it, along with skill at negotiation.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Diplomacy; Trained in Arcana, Trained in Nature, Trained in Occultism, or Trained in Religion

feats.name: Pactbound Dedication
feats.description: You befriended one of the last saumen kar and were infused with a fraction of their power, granting you the potential to become a guardian of their ancient pact.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Nature; Erutaki or Giant language, must have earned the trust of a saumen kar who initiates you into the archetype

feats.name: Pain is Temporary
feats.description: Sangpotshi practitioners know that suffering is a fleeting sensation that ultimately strengthens the soul and prepares it for a new reincarnation.

feats.name: Pain Tolerance
feats.description: You gain resistance to mental damage equal to half your level and a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects that would make you clumsy, drained, or enfeebled.
feats.source: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg pg. 79

feats.name: Paired Link
feats.description: You break a trinket, such as a lodestone or jade pendant, in two, creating a sympathetic link between the halves that bridges distance.

feats.name: Paired Shots
feats.description: Your shots hit simultaneously.

feats.name: Palatine Detective Dedication
feats.description: You have delved into the study of the occult and divine, becoming exceptionally skilled at identifying when such forces are at work.

feats.name: Palatine Enchantment
feats.description: You have found yourself facing supernatural beings often and have learned how to combat them.

feats.name: Palatine Strike
feats.description: Your strikes are particularly deadly against supernatural creatures.

feats.name: Pale Horse
feats.description: Your mount becomes a grotesque creature of foreboding when you ride it.

feats.name: Palm-leaf Silhouette
feats.description: You can make yourself as flat as a paper doll or shadow.

feats.name: Panache Paragon
feats.description: You find opportunities to perform stylish feats in the moments between others' heartbeats and eye blinks.

feats.name: Pandemonium Eruption
feats.description: You unleash the pent-up chaos within you, becoming a nexus of unbridled destruction that wreaks unfettered havoc on everyone in your vicinity.

feats.name: Panic the Dead
feats.description: Vitality strikes terror in the undead.

feats.name: Pantheon Magic
feats.description: The touch of divine meddling still runs through you, whether you want it or not.

feats.name: Paradoxical Mystery
feats.description: The truest depths of your divine mystery are fathomless and contradictory, granting you changing powers that even you can barely begin to fathom.

feats.name: Paragon Battle Medicine
feats.description: You've learned the modern uses of Battle Medicine originated by Kassi Aziril.

feats.name: Paragon Companion
feats.description: Your construct companion has reached the pinnacle of form and function.
notes: You have reached the pinnacle of necromantic science, creating a mechanical zombie unlike any the world has seen before.

feats.name: Paragon's Guard
feats.description: Once you’ve had a moment to set your stance, you always have your shield ready without a thought.

feats.name: Parallel Breakthrough
feats.description: You expand your mind beyond your area of psychic specialty.

feats.name: Paralyzing Jewel
feats.description: Your dedication to the ideals of art and beauty allows your head gem to access the power of the waning moon.

feats.name: Paralyzing Slash
feats.description: Your enzymes can paralyze your foes rather than merely making it harder for them to heal their wounds.

feats.name: Parry and Riposte
feats.description: Your parries and finishers allow you to riposte with the slightest provocation.

feats.name: Parthenogenic Hatchling
feats.description: You were hatched from an unfertilized egg during hard times for your people, and are a biological copy of your mother.

feats.name: Parting Shot
feats.description: You jump back and make a quick shot that catches your opponent by surprise.

feats.name: Pass Through
feats.description: Filtering your form through the substance of an object, you can pass through walls, doors, and more.

feats.name: Past Life
feats.description: Flashes of your life become clearer and more frequent, allowing you to apply skills you developed while alive.

feats.name: Patch Job
feats.description: You can patch up damage to an object with a quick fix.

feats.name: Pathfinder Agent Dedication
feats.description: You're a field agent of the globe-trotting Pathfinder Society.

feats.name: Patron Reborn
feats.description: You have fully realized the ability to serve as a patron.

feats.name: Patron's Breadth
feats.description: You can cast more spells each day.

feats.name: Patron's Glamour
feats.description: Gathering up threads of your old connection, you can temporarily disguise yourself as your missing patron.

feats.name: Patron's Truth
feats.description: You have mastered the greatest secrets of your patron’s magic and learned a fundamental truth about your patron, even if their identity still remains a mystery.

feats.name: Patron's Whisper
feats.description: You gain the ability to speak to your charge over great distances.

feats.name: Patron’s Claim
feats.description: Your patron partially manifests itself through your familiar to claim a foe’s power for its own.

feats.name: Patron’s Presence
feats.description: Your patron can direct its attention through your familiar, and its mere presence becomes an ominous weight on the minds of other beings to distract them and blot out their magic.

feats.name: Peafowl Stance
feats.description: You enter a tall and proud stance while remaining mobile, with all the grace and composure of a peafowl.

feats.name: Peafowl Strut
feats.description: You slowly move across the battlefield with dangerous grace.

feats.name: Peculiar Anatomy
feats.description: Your circulatory system contains viscous protoplasmic fluid instead of ordinary blood.

feats.name: Peer Beyond
feats.description: You have uncanny insight into the way ghosts and haunts manifest, manipulate the mind, and rejuvenate.

feats.name: Peerless Captain
feats.description: When you have an active follower in an encounter, you are quickened.

feats.name: Peerless Form
feats.description: Your mastery over your body is perfect enough to hold back the ravages of time.

feats.name: Peerless Mascot Companion
feats.description: As veterans of countless battles, you and your companion share an indomitable strength.

feats.name: Peerless Warden
feats.description: Your mastery of primal magic has given you access to the greatest secrets of warden magic.

feats.name: Pei Zing Adept
feats.description: You’re practiced at timing preparations to the point where you can treat multiple patients at once.
feats.prerequisites: Prepare Elemental Medicine; Expert in Crafting, Herbalism Lore, or Expert in Medicine

feats.name: Pelagic Aptitude
feats.description: You've learned special tricks to survive underwater.

feats.name: Penetrating Fire
feats.description: You blast a bullet through one foe and into another.

feats.name: Penetrating Projectile
feats.description: Your projectiles are unhindered by flesh and bone.

feats.name: Penetrating Shot
feats.description: You shoot clear through an intervening creature to hit your prey.

feats.name: Pennant of Victory
feats.description: You wave your banner in victory, signaling to your allies that the fight is won.

feats.name: Peony's Flourish
feats.description: As you spin around and create wide arching circles with your fans, you manifest a mosaic of peonies that confuses your enemies.

feats.name: Perfect Clarity
feats.description: You burn out all your rage to ensure that your attack lands and your mind remains free.

feats.name: Perfect Debilitation
feats.description: You have perfected the formulas for bombs that impede your enemies.

feats.name: Perfect Distraction
feats.description: You use clever tactics to mislead your foes as you sneak away.
notes: Provocator Dedication; expert in any kind of weapon or unarmed attack
Your experience has made you better with a wide variety of weapons.

feats.name: Perfect Dive
feats.description: You dive into the water with exceptional skill and connection to the waves, urging the water itself to cushion your fall.
feats.source: Absalom, City of Lost Omens pg. 394, Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement pg. 4
feats.source_book: Absalom, City of Lost Omens pg. 394, Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement

feats.name: Perfect Encore
feats.description: You develop another incredible creation.

feats.name: Perfect Finisher
feats.description: You focus your panache into an impeccable assault.

feats.name: Perfect Form Control
feats.description: Thanks to magic and muscle memory, you can stay in your alternate shapes indefinitely.

feats.name: Perfect Ki Adept
feats.description: You gain the appropriate ki spell for your School of Perfection.

feats.name: Perfect Ki Exemplar
feats.description: You gain an appropriate ki spell for your School of Perfection: unblinking flame emblem for Unblinking Flame, unbreaking wave barrier for Unbreaking Waves, unfolding wind blitz for Unfolding Wind, or untwisting iron augmentation for Untwisting Iron.

feats.name: Perfect Ki Expert
feats.description: You gain an appropriate ki spell for your School of Perfection: unblinking flame aura for Unblinking Flame, unbreaking wave vapor for Unbreaking Waves, unfolding wind buffet for Unfolding Wind, or untwisting iron roots for Untwisting Iron.

feats.name: Perfect Ki Grandmaster
feats.description: You gain an appropriate ki spell for your School of Perfection: unblinking flame ignition for Unblinking Flame, unbreaking wave containment for Unbreaking Waves, unfolding wind crash for Unfolding Wind, or untwisting iron pillar for Untwisting Iron.
feats.prerequisites: Perfect Ki Expert or Perfect Ki Exemplar

feats.name: Perfect Pitch
feats.description: Your ear for harmony has enhanced your ability with musical magic.

feats.name: Perfect Protection
feats.description: Your armor protects you from the deadliest hits.

feats.name: Perfect Readiness
feats.description: You prepare to fire with an instinctive speed and muscle memory that go beyond your conscious thoughts.

feats.name: Perfect Resistance
feats.description: You gain resistance equal to half your level to all damage from effects with the appropriate trait for your School of Perfection: fire for Unblinking Flame, water for Unbreaking Waves, air for Unfolding Wind, and earth for Untwisting Iron.

feats.name: Perfect Shot
feats.description: After watching the motions of combat with incredible intensity and precision, you fire at your prey at the perfect moment to deliver maximum pain.

feats.name: Perfect Strike
feats.description: You call upon insight from your training to recover accuracy.

feats.name: Perfect Truths
feats.description: Your knowledge of Razmir’s faith and doctrines has grown.

feats.name: Perfect Weaponry
feats.description: You gain the Monastic Weaponry monk class feat.

feats.name: Perfected Evaluations
feats.description: You instantly assess the strengths and weaknesses of the enemy forces.

feats.name: Perfected Gamtu
feats.description: Your hat embodies the illusory grandeur of a master dokkaebi.

feats.name: Perfection's Path
feats.description: Choose one saving throw (Fortitude, Reflex, or Will) in which you’re an expert.
feats.prerequisites: Monk Dedication; expert in at least one saving throw

feats.name: Performer's Treatment
feats.description: With your experience as a performer, you can keep a set of medical tools handy at all times.
feats.prerequisites: Expert in Medicine; Expert in Performance

feats.name: Perfume Cloud
feats.description: You issue a cloud of your fragrance like a full-body sneeze—but one that might save your life as the powerful perfume makes your attackers' eyes sting and water.

feats.name: Peri Magic
feats.description: The shimmering embers of the righteous peris crackle at your fingertips.

feats.name: Pernicious Poison
feats.description: You can add a toxic additive to an alchemical poison to make it splash poison on its target.

feats.name: Perpetual Breadth
feats.description: You have expanded your supply of near-infinite items.

feats.name: Perplex
feats.description: You weave a confusing web of half-truths, conspiracy theories, and plausible motives to goad an enemy into lashing out wildly.

feats.name: Persistent Boost
feats.description: Your innovation sets foes on fire, covers them in acid, leaves barbed thorns behind, or otherwise deals persistent damage to your foes that sticks around long after you deliver your boosted attack.

feats.name: Persistent Creation
feats.description: You can cast creation as an innate spell at will, choosing whether it is arcane or primal each time.

feats.name: Persistent Mutagen
feats.description: You've trained your physical form to remain stable.

feats.name: Persistent Odor
feats.description: Your pheromones now linger on creatures.

feats.name: Person of Interest
feats.description: Sometimes, you need to follow the leads as they come in the middle of an investigation.

feats.name: Pervasive Superstition
feats.description: You steep yourself in superstition and practice ancient orc mental exercises for shrugging off the effects of magic.

feats.name: Pesh Skin
feats.description: Your plant form sprouts hundreds of spines, and your blood causes mild hallucinations in creatures exposed to it.
feats.source: Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad pg. 78

feats.name: Pet
feats.description: You have a loyal pet

feats.name: Petal District Socialite
feats.description: Through your experience with the many parties held nightly in clubs and estates within the Petal District, you understand the way rumors and gossip circulate during social events.

feats.name: Petal Step
feats.description: You have a mastery over your own movement and can distribute your weight to dance lightly over the ground like petals drifting on the water.

feats.name: Petrified Skin
feats.description: You were the target of some petrifying effect, and an element of that stony gaze has remained with you.
feats.prerequisites: You have been petrified by an enemy.

feats.name: Petrifying Gaze Mimicry
feats.description: Either by witnessing or surviving the petrifying stare of a medusa or similar creature, you have learned to petrify those you lock eyes with.
feats.prerequisites: Wild Mimic Dedication; you have attempted a saving throw against a creature’s petrifying gaze or a similar ability or have identified a creature with such an ability in combat

feats.name: PFS Feat 1
feats.description: You've trained yourself to maintain a strong sense of direction at all times, even when you can't use a compass or see the sky above.
feats.prerequisites: Pathfinder Agent Dedication; Trained in Survival

feats.name: PFS Feat 10
feats.description: You rush forward and latch onto your enemy with talons of hungering fire.

feats.name: PFS Feat 11
feats.description: You're renowned for your ability to recover important items and sometimes that requires creating a stand-in.
feats.prerequisites: Expert in Crafting; Pathfinder Agent Dedication

feats.name: PFS Feat 12
feats.description: Identifying a creature is the first step in finding its weaknesses.

feats.name: PFS Feat 13
feats.description: You dart towards your opponent, seeking to stab them and slice their purse-strings with a single movement.

feats.name: PFS Feat 14
feats.description: You can use your wayfinder to store a little extra magic for when you need it most.
feats.prerequisites: Pathfinder Agent Dedication; Wayfinder Resonance Tinkerer

feats.name: PFS Feat 15
feats.description: Your Pathfinder training allows you to quickly size up numerous threats.
feats.prerequisites: Pathfinder Agent Dedication; master in a skill used to Recall Knowledge

feats.name: PFS Feat 16
feats.description: You can use your magic to transform into a small creature of a type that commonly serves as a spellcaster's familiar, potentially allowing you to safely scout for your party while retaining a degree of spellcasting ability.

feats.name: PFS Feat 17
feats.description: You feel confident that you can explain your scrolls so well that even your allies can use them.

feats.name: PFS Feat 18
feats.description: You've learned to recognize the telltale writings and other signs that indicate the presence of magical traps.

feats.name: PFS Feat 19
feats.description: You've learned to repair your equipment even in challenging circumstances, making use of what you have available.

feats.name: PFS Feat 2
feats.description: You've traversed enough untamed wilderness to gain an intuitive understanding of the dangers that exist in such places.

feats.name: PFS Feat 20
feats.description: You've spent countless hours learning to recognize and predict a creature's innate magical abilities.

feats.name: PFS Feat 21
feats.description: You've learned a few tricks to ensure you find every last magic item and interesting magical effect during your missions.

feats.name: PFS Feat 22
feats.description: You spend a lot of time focusing on your physical fitness.
feats.prerequisites: Swordmaster Dedication; Expert in Acrobatics; Expert in Athletics

feats.name: PFS Feat 23
feats.description: You place your body between your friends and your enemies, protecting your allies against oncoming attacks.

feats.name: PFS Feat 24
feats.description: You know that situations can change rapidly and might leave you needing to change equipment in the middle of combat.

feats.name: PFS Feat 25
feats.description: You keep hold of your weapons even when knocked out.

feats.name: PFS Feat 26
feats.description: Pathfinders are trained to help each other be safe and successful, and you know how to jolt your allies back to their senses.
feats.prerequisites: Master in Medicine; Pathfinder Agent Dedication

feats.name: PFS Feat 27
feats.description: You gain the transcribe moment focus spell.

feats.name: PFS Feat 28
feats.description: You are adept at using the unusual resonance of your wayfinder to aid in any deceptions you might employ.
feats.prerequisites: Pathfinder Agent Dedication; Wayfinder Resonance Tinkerer

feats.name: PFS Feat 29
feats.description: You've slept many nights in your armor and gotten used to it.
feats.prerequisites: Swordmaster Dedication; trained in light, medium, or heavy armor

feats.name: PFS Feat 3
feats.description: A good getaway requires a good distraction.

feats.name: PFS Feat 30
feats.description: You learn how to apply your extensive knowledge of certain creatures using the lore you know.
feats.prerequisites: Master in Arcana, Master in Crafting, Master in Nature, Master in Occultism, or Master in Religion; Scrollmaster Dedication

feats.name: PFS Feat 31
feats.description: You've studied the many ways that ancient architects and spellcasters protected their possessions, such as by implementing deadly traps and curses, and learned some magic to counteract them.

feats.name: PFS Feat 32
feats.description: Pathfinders are often asked to study the intricacies of ancient history under less than ideal conditions, and you've trained your brain to make the most of your limited time.

feats.name: PFS Feat 33
feats.description: You've become comfortable in using weapons that you might not have picked up before training with the Swords.

feats.name: PFS Feat 34
feats.description: You've mastered the art of deceiving others about your area of study.
feats.prerequisites: master in a skill with the Recall Knowledge action; Scrollmaster Dedication

feats.name: PFS Feat 35
feats.description: After extensive practice with the Swords, you're comfortable enough with your shield to move at a normal pace with it raised.

feats.name: PFS Feat 36
feats.description: You recover the energy of a recently disrupted spell, allowing you to cast it again as long as you do so immediately before the energy dissipates.

feats.name: PFS Feat 37
feats.description: You can create a number of powerful shielding runes that bolster your defenses against damaging spells by absorbing the worst of the energy to keep you safe.

feats.name: PFS Feat 38
feats.description: Competitive Eater
notes: Gorging on flesh after your stomach is full fortifies you, making you hardy enough to withstand more punishment.

feats.name: PFS Feat 39
feats.description: You've mastered useful spells that let you find hidden truths others might miss.

feats.name: PFS Feat 4
feats.description: Surprisingly agile even in a breastplate and carrying a full rucksack, Marcos Farabellus developed this evasive maneuver during one of his many adventures.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Acrobatics; trained in medium armor

feats.name: PFS Feat 40
feats.description: You've learned a variety of methods for helping allies who are bleeding, burning, or otherwise suffering persistent damage, from brushing off acid to patting down fires.

feats.name: PFS Feat 41
feats.description: You paid attention during your studies and remember the right way to attack various creatures.
feats.prerequisites: Swordmaster Dedication; expert in a skill used to Recall Knowledge

feats.name: PFS Feat 42
feats.description: You've studied survival skills with the School of Swords experts and are comfortable spending long periods of time in the wilderness.

feats.name: PFS Feat 43
feats.description: Your time as a Pathfinder has taught you how to improvise, and you've learned to make use of the resources you find in the field.

feats.name: PFS Feat 44
feats.description: You often find that the best way to win a conflict means avoiding a fight in the first place.

feats.name: PFS Feat 45
feats.description: You've spent time training in your armor alongside other agents from the Swords.
feats.prerequisites: Swordmaster Dedication; trained in light, medium, or heavy armor

feats.name: PFS Feat 46
feats.description: You lift your enemy into the air before bringing them crashing down with a fiery detonation.

feats.name: PFS Feat 47
feats.description: Your ranged attacks push your foes away from your allies.

feats.name: PFS Feat 48
feats.description: You call upon the power of your spells to protect you from the worst of the magic headed your way.

feats.name: PFS Feat 49
feats.description: You're so focused on your allies that you can help them to recenter when something would take them out of the fight.

feats.name: PFS Feat 5
feats.description: You've gained an intuitive sense about creatures.

feats.name: PFS Feat 50
feats.description: When a foe Casts a Spell and you can see its manifestations, you can use your magic to disrupt it.
feats.prerequisites: Spellmaster Dedication; master in spell attack rolls and spell DCs

feats.name: PFS Feat 51
feats.description: You dart around the battlefield, casting spells while you carefully make melee attacks with your weapon.

feats.name: PFS Feat 52
feats.description: To protect the knowledge within your mind from intrusion or erasure, you ward yourself against a mental attack.

feats.name: PFS Feat 53
feats.description: When you absorb a spell using the Absorb Spell reaction, you utilize secret, techniques that allow you to retain the power and knowledge necessary to cast the spell repeatedly—potentially for a greater period of time.

feats.name: PFS Feat 54
feats.description: You fully protect your allies from the worst blows.

feats.name: PFS Feat 6
feats.description: You were schooled on a wide variety of creatures you might encounter.

feats.name: PFS Feat 7
feats.description: You're familiar with the danger that lingering spirits can pose to an unwary adventurer.

feats.name: PFS Feat 8
feats.description: Pathfinder agents serve as members of diverse teams with complementary skill sets.

feats.name: PFS Feat 9
feats.description: You visually or audibly warn your companions of danger, granting them a +1 circumstance bonus to their initiative rolls.

feats.name: Phalanx Breaker
feats.description: You know that to take out an enemy formation, you must punch a hole through its center.

feats.name: Phalanx Formation (Guardian)
feats.description: You know how to clear a line of fire for your allies.

feats.name: Phalanx Formation (Knight Vigilant)
feats.description: You know how to clear a line of fire for your allies.

feats.name: Phantom Charm
feats.description: You can harness the spiritual connection in your blood, allowing you to shift the negative threads of fate.

feats.name: Phantom Orchestra
feats.description: An easy way to trick others is to throw your voice into a nearby forest or thicket to make strange noises.

feats.name: Phantom Resemblance
feats.description: Your can now draw from experience to wear any face you remember.

feats.name: Phantom Visage
feats.description: You cloak yourself in illusions to mask your nature.

feats.name: Phase Bullet
feats.description: You can aim your bullets not just in three normal dimensions, but in additional dimensions as well, removing all obstacles between you and your target.
notes: Strike a foe and restrain them.

feats.name: Phase Out
feats.description: Your eidolon phases out of reality slightly, gaining resistance to all damage (except force and negative) equal to half your level.

feats.name: Phenom's Verve
feats.description: You're possessed with an incredibly vivacious spark that separates you not only from ordinary gun users and gunslingers, but even from other phenoms.

feats.name: Pheromonal Message
feats.description: Your communicative pheromones are particularly well developed from years of living with other athamarus.

feats.name: Phoenix's Flight
feats.description: You and your familiar learn the secrets of elemental fire, allowing you to merge together to become a legendary creature—a phoenix.

feats.name: Pick Up the Pace
feats.description: You lead by example and can help others push themselves beyond their normal limits.

feats.name: Pickpocket
feats.description: Trained in Thievery
feats.campaign: Steal or Palm an Object more effectively

feats.name: Pied Piping
feats.description: You learn the pied piping composition spell, which enables you to control the actions of weak-minded individuals.

feats.name: Pierce the Darkness
feats.description: Your eyes have adapted to see in all circumstances, even without the assistance of light.

feats.name: Pierce the Light
feats.description: You tear the veil between the Material Plane and its shadow.

feats.name: Piercing Critical
feats.description: Your attacks with your preferred weapons deal especially devastating wounds.

feats.name: Piercing Doom
feats.description: You have grown accustomed to the curse that comes with your blessing, able to channel its effects through your attacks and forestall your own demise.

feats.name: Piercing Quills
feats.description: Your quills can dig particularly deep into the flesh of your opponents.

feats.name: Pilgrim's Token
feats.description: You carry a small token of protection from a site holy to your faith.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Religion; follower of a specific religion

feats.name: Pinch Time
feats.description: One of your parents has a human life span and another an elven life span, with your own somewhere between.

feats.name: Pinning Fire
feats.description: You target your opponent's clothing, equipment, or loose flesh with a series of piercing projectiles to pin them to the ground or a nearby surface.

feats.name: Pinpoint Poisoner
feats.description: Unsuspecting targets are especially vulnerable to your poisons.

feats.name: Pirouette
feats.description: You twirl gracefully, spinning on one foot to evade your opponent's attack.

feats.name: Pistol Phenom Dedication
feats.description: You catch an opponent off-guard by spinning your weapon.
feats.prerequisites: trained in at least one type of one-handed firearm; Trained in Deception; Trained in Performance

feats.name: Pistol Twirl
feats.description: Your quick gestures and flair for performance distract your opponent, leaving it vulnerable to your follow-up attacks.

feats.name: Pistolero's Challenge
feats.description: With a stern call, carefully chosen barb, or some other challenging declaration, you demand your foe's attention in a duel.

feats.name: Piston Punch
feats.description: You lash out with a powerful punch, extending your dynamo to a greater length in order to attack two creatures in a row.

feats.name: Pit of Snakes
feats.description: Mundane serpents obey your summons.

feats.name: Pitborn
feats.description: Your blood bears the mark of a demon.

feats.name: Pivot Strike
feats.description: You Leap and then Strike using your staff.
feats.source: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 75
feats.source_book: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

feats.name: Plague Rat
feats.description: Your bites carry a cursed infection.

feats.name: Plague Sniffer
feats.description: You can sniff out the pungent tang of disease.

feats.name: Planar Resilience
feats.description: Your body has adapted to the variety of harsh climes that you encounter in your travels.

feats.name: Planar Sidestep
feats.description: You shift your body briefly between planes, reducing the damage from your foe's attack.

feats.name: Planar Survival
feats.description: Master in Survival
feats.campaign: Use Survival to Subsist on different planes

feats.name: Planar Traveler
feats.description: You concentrate on the spaces between planes, allowing you to enhance your movement for a short period.

feats.name: Plane Hop
feats.description: The spaces between planes are obvious to you, and you can use them to move in unexpected ways.

feats.name: Plane Step
feats.description: You temporarily move just beyond the threshold of the current plane, granting you concealment until the end of your next turn.

feats.name: Plane-Stepping Dash
feats.description: You move with magical swiftness, treading between planar boundaries to shorten your journey.

feats.name: Plant Banner
feats.description: You plant your banner to inspire your allies to hold the line.

feats.name: Plant Empathy
feats.description: You have a connection to flora that allows you to communicate with them on a rudimentary level.

feats.name: Plant Evidence
feats.description: You can put a single item you’re holding of light or negligible Bulk onto a person without them noticing by succeeding at a Thievery check against their Perception DC.

feats.name: Plant Shape
feats.description: You can take the shape of a plant creature.

feats.name: Plant Soul Siblings
feats.description: You’re now able to connect with the spirits present in the greenery of the natural world.

feats.name: Plate in Treasure
feats.description: Precious metal flows from your fingers to plate an object.

feats.name: Play Dead
feats.description: You lie prone and pretend to be an ordinary skeleton.

feats.name: Play to the Crowd
feats.description: You have a knack for knowing just how to manipulate groups by saying exactly what needs to be said.

feats.name: Plot the Future
feats.description: You spend 10 minutes in contemplation to uncannily predict how events will play out.

feats.name: Pluck From the Sky
feats.description: The skies are your rightful place, and you will not suffer another to occupy them.

feats.name: Plum Deluge
feats.description: Fists of the Ruby Phoenix
feats.source: Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain pg. 76
feats.prerequisites: You have learned to saturate an area with poison.
notes: Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients pg. 78
Your mask carries the protective instincts of White Bull, the Horn Forger, and no one will hurt your allies while you have anything to say about it.

feats.name: Plumekith
feats.description: Your blood soars with the power of the magnificent garuda, proud and stoic avian warriors.

feats.name: Plummeting Roll
feats.description: Through nimble movements, a graceful landing, and a bit of luck, you can manage to fall from spectacular heights without a scratch.

feats.name: Point Blank Stance
feats.description: You take aim to pick off nearby enemies quickly.

feats.name: Poison Resistance
feats.description: Your affinity for the natural world grants you protection against some of its dangers.

feats.name: Poison Weapon
feats.description: You apply a contact poison or injury poison to the required weapon; if you have a free hand, you can Interact to draw a poison as part of this action.

feats.name: Poisoned Sticks and Stones
feats.description: You prepare your sling stones with small, edged grooves to enable them to deliver poison, and you have learned other techniques for poisoning your weapons.

feats.name: Polearm Tricks
feats.description: Your connection to rune magic has revealed tricks to make polearms deadlier in your hands.

feats.name: Polished Distraction
feats.description: You take pride in the sheen of your armor, and how useful it is at distracting your enemies when you reflect light from it.

feats.name: Political Acumen
feats.description: You have spent years following the political maneuvering and secret alliances of elves in Iadara.
feats.source: Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt pg. 80

feats.name: Political Virtuoso
feats.description: Your mastery of the politics of your home nation is profound.
feats.source: Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt pg. 80

feats.name: Pollinate
feats.description: As you’re hit, you emit a dense cloud of pollen, obscuring you from sight.
feats.source: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 70

feats.name: Polymorphic Escape
feats.description: Practical Magic
feats.prerequisites: You polymorph into a tiny vermin, a cloud of leaves, or another shape that allows you to escape.
notes: +1 to Magic checks, use Magic instead of Engineering

feats.name: Ponpoko
feats.description: No true tanuki would ever be caught without a musical instrument to play under the full moon.

feats.name: Ponpoko-pon!
feats.description: Whether it’s because you play especially vigorously or are simply off-key, the music of your belly drum can physically wound your foes.

feats.name: Portentous Spell
feats.description: Your spellcasting is rife with strange lights, mild fumes, and other captivating effects that befuddle your foes.

feats.name: Portents of the Haruspex
feats.description: Although the divinations of a haruspex traditionally require organs and lengthy interpretation, you've learned how to glimpse the future in a more simple fashion by reading creatures' wounds.

feats.name: Positioning Assault
feats.description: With punishing blows, you force your opponent into position.

feats.name: Positive Luminance
feats.description: You learn how to create a luminous reservoir of positive energy that you can use to punish undead who dare attack you.

feats.name: Potent Nectar
feats.description: Your nectar becomes particularly dangerous.
feats.source: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 70

feats.name: Pounce Mimicry
feats.description: You have mastered the art of stalking your prey and striking when they are most vulnerable.
feats.prerequisites: Wild Mimic Dedication; you have seen a creature use an ability that lets them Stride and Strike as a single action (such as Pounce) or have identified a creature with such an ability in combat

feats.name: Pouncing Transformation
feats.description: By transforming as you move, you attack from odd angles.
feats.prerequisites: Werecreature Dedication; your animal shape has a land Speed only

feats.name: Pounding Leap
feats.description: You use the momentum of your massive fists to launch yourself through the air.

feats.name: Powder Punch Stance
feats.description: You infuse your handwraps with black powder.
feats.source: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg pg. 79

feats.name: Power Slide
feats.description: You throw the vehicle into a barely controlled skid, turning sharply to evade pursuit or bring your weapons to bear.

feats.name: Powerful Guts
feats.description: Your stomach is particularly durable.

feats.name: Powerful Lash
feats.description: You've learned to swing your lash ever faster and in wider arcs.

feats.name: Powerful Leap
feats.description: Expert in Athletics

feats.name: Powerful Shove
feats.description: You can push larger foes around with your attack.

feats.name: Powerful Sneak
feats.description: You’ve learned to exploit your enemies’ openings.

feats.name: Practiced Brawn
feats.description: You're accustomed to long days filled with hard physical labor.

feats.name: Practiced Guidance
feats.description: You can better Aid your crop.

feats.name: Practiced Opposition
feats.description: You've battled more undead than you care to remember, often with little time to prepare between foes, forcing you to rely on experience and muscle memory to guide your blade.

feats.name: Practiced Paddler
feats.description: You’re skilled at navigating bogs and marshes, even for a paddler shoony.

feats.name: Practiced Reflexes
feats.description: You snap out a quick series of signals or commands that urge your allies to act with alacrity.

feats.name: Practiced Reloads
feats.description: You've mastered the reloading techniques and methods usually practiced by only the most dedicated firearm professionals.

feats.name: Prairie Rider
feats.description: You grew up riding your clan's shaggy ponies and riding dogs.

feats.name: Prayer Attack
feats.description: You’ve mastered the signature assassination style of the Red Mantis.

feats.name: Prayer-Touched Weapon
feats.description: You coat a weapon you're wielding in prayers and the divine power given to you by your deity.
feats.prerequisites: divine spells, you follow a good-aligned deity

feats.name: Precious Alloys
feats.description: Your unarmed attacks gain the properties of special materials.

feats.name: Precious Munitions
feats.description: You mix flecks of precious materials you've gathered on your journeys into your custom bullets to bring out those properties in your temporary ammunition.

feats.name: Precise Debilitations
feats.description: You attack with great precision.

feats.name: Precise Finisher
feats.description: Even when your foe avoids your Confident Finisher, you can still hit a vital spot.

feats.name: Precise Hooks
feats.description: Your body is a varied and deadly weapon.

feats.name: Predator's Growl
feats.description: You give a throaty growl to attempt to Demoralize a creature you just found.

feats.name: Predator's Pounce
feats.description: You close the distance to your prey in a blur, pouncing on the creature before it can react.

feats.name: Predatory Claws
feats.description: Your nails grow into sharp claws, perfect for seizing and tearing your prey.

feats.name: Predict Weather
feats.description: By observing often subtle indicators in the environment, you may try to predict the day's weather.

feats.name: Predictable!
feats.description: By observing an opponent, you can get a sense of their next move and prepare to counter or resist it.

feats.name: Predictive Purchase
feats.description: You have just the thing for the situation.

feats.name: Predictive Purchase (Rogue)
feats.description: You somehow manage to have just the thing for the situation.

feats.name: Predispose Settlement
feats.description: You spend 7 days of downtime seeding propaganda through a settlement with a population of 2,500 or fewer.
feats.prerequisites: Field Propagandist Dedication; Expert in Deception

feats.name: Preemptive Reconfiguration
feats.description: Your body anticipates the blow.

feats.name: Premonition of Avoidance
feats.description: Your deity grants you a moment’s foresight.

feats.name: Premonition of Clarity
feats.description: Your deity sends you a vision of faith to steel you against mental attacks.

feats.name: Preparation
feats.description: You survey the battlefield, calculating the possibilities.

feats.name: Prepare Elemental Medicine
feats.description: You diagnose your patient, then prepare and administer a dose of elemental medicine to a creature.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Crafting, Herbalism Lore, or Trained in Medicine

feats.name: Prepare Papers
feats.description: You're practiced at creating forgeries that establish your outlaw credentials or augment your legal authority.
feats.source: Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg pg. 78
feats.prerequisites: Alkenstar Agent Dedication; Master in Society

feats.name: Prescient Consumable
feats.description: Prescient Planner
feats.campaign: Procure consumable items with Prescient Planner

feats.name: Prescient Planner
feats.description: Quick Recovery (Kingdom)
feats.prerequisites: Procure a piece of adventuring gear
notes: +4 to checks to end ongoing harmful events

feats.name: Preserve the Horde
feats.description: The act of summoning additional undead minions doesn't make you lose the focus required to maintain the animation of your horde.

feats.name: Preternatural Parry
feats.description: You snap your weapon or limb into place to ward off harm at the last second.

feats.name: Preternatural Polish
feats.description: Armor you polish shines with righteous grace.

feats.name: Prevailing Position
feats.description: Your flexibility with stances lets you sacrifice their benefits to better protect yourself.

feats.name: Preventative Treatment
feats.description: You provide preventative remedies and care to help your patient fight off future maladies.

feats.name: Pride Hunter
feats.description: You avoid the attention of creatures focused on your allies.

feats.name: Pride in Arms
feats.description: With a shout of triumph, you grant inspiration to an ally fight on.

feats.name: Primadonna
feats.description: The spotlight should always be on you and you alone.

feats.name: Primal Aegis
feats.description: You surround yourself with a thick field of protective primal energy.

feats.name: Primal Breadth
feats.description: Increase the spell slots you gain from druid archetype feats by 1 for each spell rank other than your two highest druid spell slots.

feats.name: Primal Dragonblood
feats.description: A dragon with a deep connection to the natural world, such as an adamantine dragon or a horned dragon, resides somewhere on your family tree.

feats.name: Primal Evolution
feats.description: You can call upon the creatures of the wild for aid.

feats.name: Primal Focus
feats.description: Your connection to nature is particularly strong, and the spirits of nature flock around you, helping you replenish your focus.

feats.name: Primal Guardian
feats.description: You grow in power as a guardian of nature.

feats.name: Primal Howl
feats.description: Your companion can let out a howl laced with your primal magic.

feats.name: Primal Proportions
feats.description: You can mimic the sheer mass of the largest beasts.

feats.name: Primal Rampage
feats.description: You gain unfettered movement and mountain resilience as 4th-rank primal innate spells that you can cast once per day.

feats.name: Primal Summons
feats.description: Whenever you summon an ally, you can empower it with the elemental power of air, earth, fire, or water.

feats.name: Prismatic Scales
feats.description: You can cause your scales to glow with scintillating, many-hued colors, protecting you from energy while dazzling foes.

feats.name: Pristine Weapon
feats.description: Your verdant weapon can cut through the resistances of magical creatures.

feats.name: Prodigious Climber
feats.description: Your natural climbing ability and technique has made you a potent climber.

feats.name: Progenitor Lore
feats.description: You didn't just inherit your physical form from your progenitor; you also retained some of their knowledge.

feats.name: Project Persona
feats.description: Where others etch their armor to serve as a conduit for their imaginations, your vivid mind and bold personality allow you to project a more fitting persona over your lackluster armor.

feats.name: Projectile Snatching
feats.description: You pluck missiles from the air and hurl them back at their source.

feats.name: Prolific Prophet Spellcasting
feats.description: Increase the number of spells in your repertoire and the number of spell slots you gain from prophet of Kalistrade archetype feats by 1 for each spell rank other than your two highest prophet of Kalistrade spell slots.

feats.name: Promise of Pain
feats.description: You call out to an enemy in distress, promising them a future of unending pain.
feats.prerequisites: Sister of the Golden Erinys Dedication

feats.name: Propelled Leap
feats.description: Like a flying fish, you can build up momentum and launch yourself into the air.

feats.name: Propeller Attachment
feats.description: You've mastered attaching clockwork propellers to your daily quick-deploy snares that use gears.

feats.name: Propelling Sorcery
feats.description: The force of your magic can be used to propel yourself or another.

feats.name: Prophet of Kalistrade Dedication
feats.description: After induction into the ranks of the Kalistocrats, you learned the skills necessary to succeed in business and rise above others by following a set of detailed strictures.
feats.prerequisites: trained in Mercantile Lore; Trained in Society; Charisma +2

feats.name: Prophet's Lockbox
feats.description: You’ve learned secret methods that allow you to store your valuables where no one else can reach them.
notes: Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered) pg. 10, Dark Archives (Remastered) pg. 101
By expelling flames from your feet or levitating through electrical repulsion, you propel yourself through the air.

feats.name: Propulsive Mutation
feats.description: Your ostilli can fire its dart farther.

feats.name: Proteankin
feats.description: Your blood bubbles with the roiling quintessence of the Maelstrom, the infinite sea of primal chaos that long ago spawned the other Outer Planes.

feats.name: Protective Bond
feats.description: The power of your bond can protect you and your eidolon from harm.

feats.name: Protective Claws
feats.description: The chitin on your claws is so hard you can defend against attacks with them.

feats.name: Protective Cycle
feats.description: You react to harm by flowing into an elemental stance with new advantages.

feats.name: Protective Pose
feats.description: Many physical poses can fortify one’s health, but some poses need more than one body to accomplish.

feats.name: Protective Sheath
feats.description: When you lash out with your clan dagger, you keep its sheath close at hand.

feats.name: Protective Strike
feats.description: You keep harm at bay.
notes: For you, fighting is as much about style as skill.

feats.name: Protective Subroutine
feats.description: Your nanites can augment your defenses.

feats.name: Prototype Companion
feats.description: You have created a construct companion, and while it might not be an innovation, it serves as a trustworthy minion.

feats.name: Proud Mentor
feats.description: You enjoy mentoring others and take pride in your students' accomplishments.

feats.name: Proud Nail
feats.description: When a foe ignores your taunts, you make them pay.

feats.name: Proximity Alert
feats.description: You're unnaturally in tune with your surroundings and react instinctively to danger.

feats.name: Psi Burst
feats.description: With a passing thought, you direct violent psychic energies at a nearby creature.

feats.name: Psi Catastrophe
feats.description: You stop holding your power back, unleashing it all in a catastrophic blast.

feats.name: Psi Development
feats.description: Psychic Dedication
feats.campaign: You’ve found a new mental form

feats.name: Psi Strikes
feats.description: You siphon residual psychic energies from your spell into one weapon you're wielding or one of your unarmed attacks.

feats.name: Psychic Dedication
feats.description: Intelligence +2 or Charisma +2
feats.campaign: You feel something awaken within your mind

feats.name: Psychic Duelist Dedication
feats.description: Through experience and in-depth visualizations of mental battles, you've grown adept at psychic duels.
feats.prerequisites: Trained in Occultism; you have been in a psychic duel

feats.name: Psychic Rapport
feats.description: Pull Together
feats.prerequisites: Your telepathic sensitivity intensifies when you're affected by mental magic, making it easier for you to link to other creatures.

feats.name: Psychopomp Familiar
feats.description: Your familiar becomes a masked psychopomp—an extraplanar guardian of the cycle of souls.

feats.name: Public Execution
feats.description: Some rebel Firebrand cells make a practice of publicly declaring the main target of a given operation.

feats.name: Pummeling Whirlpool
feats.description: You draw water from the environs, swirling it into a rough torrent around you to punish your foes.

feats.name: Puncturing Horn
feats.description: While many kashrishi never use their horns for more than cracking open hard-shelled fruits or amplifying their psychic powers, you've practiced using yours offensively.

feats.name: Punishing Shove
feats.description: When you push a foe away, you put the entire force of your armored form into it.

feats.name: Pupil's Guard
feats.description: You're intimately familiar with your student's motions and are ready to step in at a moment's notice should anything threaten to disrupt them.

feats.name: Pure Legion Enforcer Dedication
feats.description: You are sworn to uphold the Laws of Mortality within the borders of Rahadoum.

feats.name: Purge of Moments
feats.description: All things eventually end, a truth that you can put into practice by accelerating your passage through time to shake off harmful effects.

feats.name: Purge Sins
feats.description: You call on your celestial forebears to rid your body of all toxins and impurities.

feats.name: Purify Element
feats.description: You turn an element into its purest form.

feats.name: Purifying Breeze
feats.description: You transfer the divine essence of your magic to the air around you, cleansing it of toxins.

feats.name: Purifying Spell
feats.description: You purify the water within a creature's body to cleanse them of illness.

feats.name: Push Back the Dead!
feats.description: You call to your ally and strengthen their resolve and abilities to defend against the horrific tricks undead bring to bear.

feats.name: Push it
feats.description: You can push your vehicle's performance beyond the limits envisioned by its makers.
feats.prerequisites: Trick Driver Dedication; Master in Driving Lore
notes: Cost of living expenses are reduced

feats.name: Pushing Attack
feats.description: Your eidolon has an attack that pushes away enemies.

feats.name: Pushing Wind
feats.description: As you spin and glide your fans alongside your allies, you kick up a mild wind that gently carries you all forward.

feats.name: Puzzle Solver
feats.description: You have a natural affinity for problem-solving and the unknown.

feats.name: Pyre Ant Sting
feats.description: Your swarm's stings burn with agonizing pain, much like that caused by the bright red pyre ants that make their tunnels within the parched sands of deserts like Qadira.

feats.name: Pyrophilic Recovery
feats.description: You rely on fire and extreme heat to sprout and regrow.

feats.name: Qi Center
feats.description: You center yourself with the universe and draw upon its vast power.
feats.prerequisites: Master of Many Styles; Qi Spells

feats.name: Qi Spells
feats.description: You have cultivated your qi to produce magical effects.

feats.name: Qlippoth Magic
feats.description: You've deeply studied the secrets of your blood and can force reality to act against its own wishes.

feats.name: Quadruped
feats.description: You were crafted in a form with four legs rather than two.

feats.name: Quah Bond
feats.description: You grew up among the Shoanti tribes, with the spirits watching over you, and they offer you guidance.

feats.name: Quaking Stomp
feats.description: You stomp the ground with such force that it creates a minor earthquake, with the effects of the earthquake spell.

feats.name: Quick Bomber
feats.description: You keep your bombs and bomb-related reagents in easy-to-reach pouches from which you draw without thinking.

feats.name: Quick Climb
feats.description: Master in Athletics

feats.name: Quick Coercion
feats.description: Trained in Intimidation

feats.name: Quick Disguise
feats.description: Expert in Deception
feats.campaign: Set up a disguise in less time

feats.name: Quick Draw
feats.description: You draw your weapon and attack with the same motion.
feats.source: Player Core pg. 159, Guns & Gears (Remastered) pg. 112

feats.name: Quick Identification
feats.description: trained in Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion
feats.campaign: Identify Magic in 1 minute or less

feats.name: Quick Juggler
feats.description: When you start to Juggle or add another item to those you are Juggling, you add two items instead of one.
feats.source: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 74
feats.source_book: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

feats.name: Quick Jump
feats.description: Trained in Athletics
feats.campaign: High Jump or Long Jump as a single action

feats.name: Quick Positioning
feats.description: You're always ready to get the jump on your target.

feats.name: Quick Recognition
feats.description: Identify spells as a free action
feats.prerequisites: Master in Arcana, Master in Nature, Master in Occultism, or Master in Religion; Recognize Spell
notes: You have learned to setup simple crafting jobs with ease.

feats.name: Quick Recovery (Yaoguai)
feats.description: You use your command over your form to repair lifethreatening wounds.

feats.name: Quick Repair
feats.description: Trained in Crafting

feats.name: Quick Reversal
feats.description: You turn your foes’ flanking against them with a quick reverse.

feats.name: Quick Root
feats.description: You react instinctively, sprouting roots from your legs and plunging them deep into the ground to keep your footing.
feats.source: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 69

feats.name: Quick Shape
feats.description: Your instincts kick in and you take on an aggressive stance.

feats.name: Quick Shield Block
feats.description: You can bring your shield into place with hardly a thought.
feats.source: Player Core pg. 147, Player Core 2 pg. 97

feats.name: Quick Shot
feats.description: You draw your bow and attack with the same action.

feats.name: Quick Spring
feats.description: Ishii Bunji is a skilled tumbler, and learning his technique allows you to propel yourself across the battlefield faster than your enemies can react.

feats.name: Quick Squeeze
feats.description: Trained in Acrobatics

feats.name: Quick Stow
feats.description: You Interact to store one item you're holding in your cheek pouches (provided it fits).

feats.name: Quick Swim
feats.description: Master in Athletics

feats.name: Quick Unlock
feats.description: Master in Thievery

feats.name: Quick Vengeance
feats.description: Indignant at any who would strike you or your allies, you get your revenge.

feats.name: Quicken Heartbeat
feats.description: You’ve replaced your heart with one made of animated quicksilver and living adamantine.

feats.name: Quickened Attunement
feats.description: If your next action is to cast a spell that would grant your terrain attunement bonus, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (to a minimum of 1 action).

feats.name: Quickened Casting
feats.description: If your next action is to cast a cantrip or a spell that is at least 2 ranks lower than the highest-rank spell slot you have, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).
feats.source: Player Core pg. 105, Player Core 2 pg. 141

feats.name: Quickening Banner
feats.description: The sight of your banner urges your allies to strike now.

feats.name: Quicksoul
feats.description: The elemental metal in your bloodline literally courses through your veins.

feats.name: Quiet Allies
feats.description: Expert in Stealth
feats.campaign: Roll a single Stealth check when sneaking with allies

feats.name: Quietus Strikes
feats.description: Your connection to the Boneyard empowers you to bring death to all forms of life and unlife, exploiting their weaknesses.

feats.name: Quill Spray
feats.description: You can launch a mass of quills at opponents.
feats.prerequisites: Quillcoat Shisk heritage or quill unarmed attack

feats.name: Rabid Sprint
feats.description: You run on all fours as fast as you can.

feats.name: Radiant Armament
feats.description: Your blessed armament radiates power, further enhancing your chosen weapon.

feats.name: Radiant Burst
feats.description: Your skin glows with intensity.

feats.name: Radiant Circuitry
feats.description: Your biological circuitry emits light like a torch, casting bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet).

feats.name: Radiate Glory
feats.description: Your inherent glory radiates into the world with all the divine power of the celestial planes for 1 minute.

feats.name: Ragdya's Dance
feats.description: Even when faced with deadly danger, you fight with the rapturous laughter of Ragdya in your heart and can play impossible tricks.

feats.name: Ragdya's Revelry
feats.description: You dart away from your foe's attack with a casual laugh, using the opening to cause mischief.

feats.name: Raging Athlete
feats.description: Physical obstacles can't hold back your fury.

feats.name: Raging Intimidation
feats.description: Your fury fills your foes with fear.

feats.name: Raging Stories
feats.description: When you Offer a Story about wild chances and fighting against all odds, you can wrap yourself in spectral flame in place of the normal benefits.

feats.name: Raging Thrower
feats.description: Thrown weapons become especially deadly in your fury.

feats.name: Rain of Bolts
feats.description: Throughout the day, your body produces powerful projectiles within your chassis.

feats.name: Rain of Razors
feats.description: Razor-sharp slivers of metal fall from the sky.

feats.name: Rain of Rust
feats.description: You conjure a red raincloud in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet, which pours rust-colored rain directly below it.

feats.name: Rain-Scribe Mobility
feats.description: You surround yourself in a mantle of Rain-Scribe magic and Stride twice; difficult terrain does not reduce your Speed, even if the difficult terrain has been manipulated by magic.

feats.name: Rain-Scribe Sustenance
feats.description: You use the power of your Rain-Scribe magic to draw on a stream of primal power for sustenance.

feats.name: Raise a Tome
feats.description: You raise up the book you're holding and flip it open to defend yourself and expedite your studies.

feats.name: Raise Haft
feats.description: You know how to use the haft of larger weapons to block your enemies' attacks.

feats.name: Raise Island
feats.description: You churn the sea and fish up stone so your enemies together can receive their punishment.
feats.prerequisites: born of the bones of the earth or restless as the tides

feats.name: Raise Menhir
feats.description: You raise a druidic monument, such as a standing stone or warding tree, from the ground, creating a powerful primal ward that blocks other types of magic.

feats.name: Raise Symbol
feats.description: You present your religious symbol emphatically.

feats.name: Rakshasa Magic
feats.description: You remember the secrets of your rakshasa forebears and know how to apply those memories to get results.

feats.name: Rakshasa Ravaged
feats.description: You and your family know well the ravages of rakshasa predations, and you know how to best defend yourself against their growing corruption in the world.

feats.name: Rally Support
feats.description: You often rely on the local populace for supplies and shelter.
feats.prerequisites: Guerrilla Dedication; trained in either Diplomacy or Intimidation

feats.name: Rallying Anthem
feats.description: You learn the rallying anthem composition cantrip, which protects you and allies.

feats.name: Rallying Banner
feats.description: Your banner waves high, reminding your allies that the fight can still be won.

feats.name: Rallying Charge
feats.description: When you lead your allies to the front lines, you leave no one behind.

feats.name: Rallying Cry
feats.description: You call out to your allies and push them to give their all in combat.

feats.name: Rampaging Ferocity
